seoTitle: Add a Reset Filters Button in Power BI (Bookmark Method)
metaDescription: Give report viewers a one-click way to restore original filters in Power BI Desktop using the bookmark method, explained step by step.
intro: When viewers apply several filters to a Power BI report, getting back to the default view can be a hassle without a dedicated reset option. This guide explains how to build a reset button using Power BI's bookmark feature so users can restore the original filter state with a single click. It's a small addition that makes dashboards noticeably easier to use.

steps.heading: Save a bookmark of the default view
steps.body: Before any filters are applied, create a bookmark that captures the report's original state so it can be restored later.

steps.heading: Insert a button onto the report
steps.body: Add a button to your report page that will act as the visible reset control for viewers.

steps.heading: Link the button to the bookmark
steps.body: Set the button's action to point to the bookmark you saved, so clicking it returns the report to its original filter state.

steps.heading: Customize the button's appearance
steps.body: Adjust the button's label, icon, and styling so it's clearly recognizable as a reset control within your report layout.

steps.heading: Test the reset behavior
steps.body: Apply several filters, click the button, and confirm the report reliably returns to the saved default view before sharing it with others.

faq.question: Do I need Power BI Premium to add a reset filters button?
faq.answer: No, bookmarks and buttons are available in standard Power BI Desktop, so no premium license is required.

faq.question: Will the reset button clear filters on every page of my report?
faq.answer: It resets whatever state was captured in the bookmark, so you can scope it to the current page or the whole report depending on how you set it up.

faq.question: Can I use an icon instead of text on the reset button?
faq.answer: Yes, buttons in Power BI support custom icons, so you can use a reset-style icon instead of or alongside text.

faq.question: What happens if I change the report layout after creating the bookmark?
faq.answer: You may need to update or recreate the bookmark so it still reflects the correct default state after layout changes.

faq.question: Is the bookmark method the only way to reset filters?
faq.answer: It's the most common no-code approach; more advanced setups could use other techniques, but bookmarks are the simplest for most reports.
